name: Motorola RAZR V3
description: An instant icon with a razor-thin aluminum body. Over 130 million units sold globally, cementing its status as a pop culture symbol.

name: Samsung D500
description: A sleek slider phone with a 1.3MP camera and polyphonic ringtones that helped establish Samsung as a design-forward brand.

name: BlackBerry 7290
description: One of the first colour-screen BlackBerrys, bringing push email and BBM to a wider business audience with a comfortable full QWERTY keyboard.

name: Nokia N70
description: A 3G pocket powerhouse running Symbian, combining speed and versatility for those who preferred not to wrestle with Windows Mobile.

name: Sony Ericsson W800i
description: The first phone to carry the legendary Walkman branding, with crisp stereo sound, a dedicated music button, and bold orange-and-white design.

name: Nokia N91
description: The only Nokia phone with a hard drive — 4GB with room for 3,000 songs — and dedicated music keys for audiophiles.

name: Motorola RAZR V3i
description: An improved RAZR with a 1.2MP camera with 8x digital zoom and iTunes functionality for up to 100 songs.

name: Nokia N73
description: A media-forward phone with a large 2.4-inch display, stereo speakers, and a 3.2MP camera that appealed to multimedia fans.

name: Sony Ericsson K800i
description: The first Cyber-shot camera phone, with a 3.2MP sensor, Xenon flash, and a lens cover — it made people take camera phones seriously.

name: LG Chocolate (VX8500)
description: A sleek slider with touch-sensitive face buttons and an MP3 player. Its stylish branding helped it sell over 20 million units.

name: Samsung E250
description: The best-selling slider phone of its era, moving over 30 million units worldwide with a simple, reliable design.

name: Motorola Q
description: A slim Windows Mobile smartphone and BlackBerry rival that packed a full QWERTY keyboard into a surprisingly thin candybar body.

name: BlackBerry Pearl 8100
description: The first BlackBerry to look genuinely stylish, with a trackball and email capabilities that felt revolutionary at the time.

name: Samsung BlackJack (SGH-i607)
description: A notably slim Windows Mobile smartphone with Bluetooth 2.0 and push email, positioned as a direct BlackBerry rival.

name: Nokia N95
description: A GPS-enabled dual-slider with a 5MP camera, HSDPA, and expandable storage. So capable it might have reigned supreme if not for the iPhone.

name: Sony Ericsson W880i
description: A sleek, brushed-metal Walkman phone that was impressively thin for its time and a dream for music lovers.

name: BlackBerry Curve 8300
description: A phone with a perfect keyboard and BBM messaging that crossed over from business professionals to teens and everyday users.

name: LG Prada (KE850)
description: The first smartphone with a capacitive touchscreen, released just before the iPhone. LG later claimed Apple copied its design.

name: LG Viewty (KU990)
description: A 3G touchscreen phone with a 5MP autofocus camera and Schneider Kreuznach optics. Launched the very same month as the iPhone.

name: Apple iPhone (1st generation)
description: Released June 29, 2007. The phone that changed everything — a revolutionary multi-touch interface, desktop-class browser, and widescreen iPod in one device.
